dtcs.description: (59): High Voltage Short Circuit

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch ON (II).
Check for Temporary DTCs or DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1629 indicated?
steps.yes: Do the troubleshooting for DTC P1629.
steps.no: Go to step 3.

steps.instruction: Turn the A/C switch off.
Check the H.V. INSLT in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is about 239 k ohms or less indicated?
steps.no: Go to step 23.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU lid.
Check the high voltage cable (orange cable) connection and busbar connection on the junction board. Are the connections OK?
steps.no: Repair the connections, then go to step 41.

steps.instruction: Remove the busbars (A) from the MPI module.
Measure resistance between body ground and MPI module terminals + (A) and - (B) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the MPI module, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Remove the Y capacitor.
Measure resistance between the ground wire (A) and Y capacitor terminals + (B) and - (C) individually.
steps.no: Replace the Y capacitor, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Remove the busbar (A) and wire (B) from the high voltage contactor.
Remove the junction board terminals (C).
Measure resistance between body ground and high voltage contactor terminals + (D) and - (E) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the high voltage contactor, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the wires (A) from the bypass contactor (B).
Measure resistance between body ground and bypass contactor terminals No. 1 (C) and No. 2 (D) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the bypass contactor, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Measure resistance between body ground and wire (from bypass contactor) (A). Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the bypass contactor, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Remove the junction board.
Measure resistance between body ground and DC-DC converter terminals + (A) and - (B) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the DC-DC converter, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Disconnect MCM connector C (14P).
Measure resistance between body ground and battery module terminals + (A) and - (B) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.no: Replace the battery module, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Start the engine, and let it idle 1 minute.
Check the H.V. INSLT in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is about 239 k ohms or less indicated?
steps.no: Go to step 33.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU lid.
Remove the motor power cables (A) from the MPI module.
Measure resistance between body ground and the MPI module terminals No. 1 (A), No. 2 (B) and No. 3 (C) individually.
steps.no: Replace the MPI module, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the motor power cable (A).
Measure for resistance between body ground and the motor power cable terminals No. 1 (A), No. 2 (B) and No. 3 (C) individually.
steps.no: Replace the motor power cable, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Measure resistance between body ground and the motor stator terminals No. 1 (A), No. 2 (B) and No. 3 (C) individually.
steps.no: Replace the motor stator, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Make sure the outside temperature is above 41°F (5°C).
Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Start the engine, and let it idle 5 minutes.
Turn the A/C on, then set the temperature to 72°F (22°C).
Let the engine idle 1 minute.
Check the H.V. INSLT in the DATA LIST with the HDS, Is about 239 k ohms or less indicated?
steps.no: Temporary short detected in the high voltage parts, system is OK at this time.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Disconnect the A/C compressor power cable connector in the engine compartment.
Measure resistance between body ground and the A/C compressor power cable terminals No. 1 (A), No. 2 (B) and No. 3 (C) individually.
steps.no: Go to step 46.

steps.instruction: Remove the A/C compressor.
Drain the oil from the A/C compressor.
Measure resistance between body ground and the A/C compressor power cable connector terminals No. 1 (A), No. 2 (B) and No. 3 (C) individually.
steps.yes: Replace the A/P compressor, the A/C condenser, the A/C system lines and hoses, and the evaporator, then go to step 48.
steps.no: Replace the A/C compressor, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the A/C compressor power cables (A) from the stay, then wrap the cable ends with insulating tape (B).
Check for continuity between body ground and the A/C compressor power cable connector terminals No. 1 (C), No. 2 (D) and No. 3 (E) individually. Is there 350 k ohms or more?
steps.yes: Replace motor power cable, then go to step 48.
steps.no: Replace the A/C compressor driver, then go to step 48.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Reconnect all connectors.
Reinstall the IPU lid, then turn the battery module switch ON.
Turn the ignition switch ON (II).
Reset the MCM.
Start the engine, and let it idle 5 minutes.
Turn the A/C on, then set the temperature to 72°F (22°C).
Check for Temporary DTCs or DTCs with the HDS. Are any Temporary DTCs or DTCs indicated? are indicated, go to the indicated DTC s troubleshooting.
steps.yes: If DTC P0AA6 is indicated, a temporary short was detected in the high voltage parts; go to step 1. If any other Temporary DTCs or DTCs
steps.no: Troubleshooting is complete.

steps.instruction: Reinstall the IPU lid, then turn the battery module switch ON.
Update the MCM if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good MCM.
Start the engine, and let it idle 5 minutes.
Turn the A/C on, then set the temperature to 72°F (22°C).
Check for Temporary DTCs or DTCs with the HDS. Are any Temporary DTCs or DTCs indicated? are indicated, go to the indicated DTC s troubleshooting.
steps.yes: If DTC P0AA6 is indicated, a temporary short was detected in the high voltage parts; go to step 1. If any other Temporary DTCs or DTCs
steps.no: If the MCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the MCM was substituted, replace the original MCM.

Start the engine, drive the vehicle in the D position until the vehicle speed reaches 25 mph (40 km/h), then slow down and stop the wheels.
Turn the ignition switch OFF, restart the engine and retest-drive the vehicle in the D position until the vehicle speed reaches 25 mph (40 km/h), then slow down and stop the wheels.
Monitor the OBD status for P0706 in the DTCs/Freeze Data in A/T Mode Menu for a pass/fail. Does the result indicate PASSED?
steps.no: Return to step 6 and recheck.

dtcs.description: Problem in 1st Clutch and 1st Clutch Hydraulic Circuit

steps.instruction: Warm up the engine to normal operating temperature (the radiator fan comes on).
Make sure that the transmission is filled to the proper level, and check for fluid leaks.
Drain the ATF through a strainer. Inspect the strainer for metal debris or excessive clutch material. Does the strainer have metal debris or excessive clutch material?
steps.no: Replace the ATF, then go to step 4.

steps.instruction: Measure the line pressure. Is the line pressure within the service limit?
steps.no: Repair the ATF pump and regulator valve, or replace the transmission.

steps.instruction: Measure the 1st clutch pressure. Is the 1st clutch pressure within the service limit?
steps.no: Shift valves B and C are stuck. Repair the shift valves and hydraulic circuit, or replace the transmission.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Test-drive the vehicle for several minutes under the same conditions as those indicated by the freeze data, or drive in 1st gear in the D position at speeds over 10 mph (16 km/h) for 20 seconds. Slow down and stop the wheels.
Monitor the OBD status for P0731 in the DTCs/Freeze Data in A/T Mode Menu for a pass/fail. Does the result indicate FAILED?
steps.yes: Repair the 1st clutch, or replace the transmission.
steps.no: Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. If the tester indicates NOT COMPLETED, return to step 7 and recheck.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF.
Drain the coolant
Remove ECT sensor 1.
Allow ECT sensor 1 to cool to ambient temperature.
Note the ambient temperature.
Connect ECT sensor 1 to its 2P connector, but do not install it into the engine.
Turn the ignition switch ON (II).
Note the value of ECT SENSOR 1 quickly in the DATA LIST with the HDS.
Compare the value of ECT SENSOR 1 and the ambient temperature. Does the value of ECT SENSOR 1 differ 5.4 degrees F (3 degrees C) or more?
steps.yes: Replace ECT sensor 1, then go to step 27.
steps.no: Intermittent failure, system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the PCM.

steps.no: Go to step 19.

steps.yes: Intermittent failure, system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EGR valve and the PCM.
steps.no: Go to step 5.

steps.yes: Repair open in the wire between the EGR valve and the PCM (C13), then go to step 44.

dtcs.description: Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump (ATFP) Motor Rotor or Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Stuck

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Choose ATFP Motor Test in Miscellaneous Test Menu, and check that DTC P2797 recurs. Is DTC P2797 indicated?
steps.yes: Replace the ATFP, then go to step 3.
steps.no: Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Warm up the engine to normal operating temperature (the radiator fan comes on).
Drive the vehicle in the D position. Slow down, and stop the wheels in auto idle stop for more than 10 seconds.
Monitor the OBD status for P2797 in the DTCs/Freeze Data in A/T Mode Menu for a pass/fail. Does the result indicate PASSED?
steps.no: Return to step 1 and recheck.

Do the ATFP Motor Test again in about 10 seconds after finishing the preceding test.
